notes: Primary key; decimal(10,0) has no fractional part so INT64 is the precise and storage-efficient BigQuery type. First component of the composite merge key used by sp_mysqltobq_load.sql. Clustered first to accelerate merge lookups and point queries.
PII field. BigQuery STRING subsumes varchar(50); original length constraint enforced by pre-load schema validation.
PII field. BigQuery STRING subsumes varchar(50); original length constraint enforced by pre-load schema validation.
PII field. BigQuery STRING subsumes varchar(100); original length constraint enforced by pre-load schema validation.
PII field; nullable in source. Kept as STRING to preserve formatting (leading zeros, country codes, separators).
Direct DATE-to-DATE mapping; no timezone handling required. Useful for tenure calculations in analytics.
Job role code; used as the third clustering column to optimise analytics queries filtered or grouped by job function.
Two decimal places preserved via BigQuery NUMERIC (38 digits, 9 decimal places). FLOAT64 is avoided to prevent precision loss on financial values.
Nullable; two decimal places preserved via NUMERIC. FLOAT64 avoided for financial precision integrity.
Nullable self-referential FK; decimal(10,0) has no fractional part so INT64 is appropriate. NULL indicates a top-level employee with no manager.
Nullable; decimal(4,0) has no fractional part so INT64 is appropriate. Second clustering column to optimise department-level reporting queries common in 5gcore analytics.
Low-cardinality field (e.g. active/inactive/terminated). Fourth clustering column; filtering by status is a common analytics pattern. Pre-load validation should enforce allowed values.
Record creation timestamp from MySQL; direct TIMESTAMP-to-TIMESTAMP mapping. MySQL TIMESTAMP is UTC-normalised so no timezone conversion is required.

Partition column with DAY granularity; enables BigQuery partition pruning on incremental date-range queries. Second component of the composite merge key (employee_id + updated_date) used by sp_mysqltobq_load.sql. Nullable in source — rows with NULL updated_date land in the __NULL__ partition; pre-load validation should flag these. The 60-day retention policy should be applied as a BigQuery partition expiration on this column.
